hadith-eng: Narrated Malik:We came to the Prophet (ﷺ) and we were young men nearly of equal ages and we stayed with him for twenty nights. Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) was a very kind man and when he realized our longing for our families, he asked us about those whom we had left behind. When we informed him, he said, "Go back to your families and stay with them and teach them (religion) and order them (to do good deeds). The Prophet (ﷺ) mentioned things some of which I remembered and some I did not. Then he said, "Pray as you have seen me praying, and when it is the time of prayer, one of you should pronounce the call (Adhan) for the prayer and the eldest of you should lead the prayer
question: What qualities did the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) exhibit when interacting with young companions?
verification_reason: The text describes the Prophet's kindness and instructions to the young companions, which directly answers the question.

hadith-eng: Narrated Ibn Mas`ud:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "The (call for prayer) Adhan of Bilal should not stop anyone of you from taking his Suhur for he pronounces the Adhan in order that whoever among you is praying the night prayer, may return (to eat his Suhur) and whoever among you is sleeping, may get up, for it is not yet dawn (when it is like this)." (Yahya, the sub-narrator stretched his two index fingers side ways)
question: What is the significance of the Adhan of Bilal in relation to Suhur and night prayer?
verification_reason: The answer explains the significance of Bilal's Adhan in relation to Suhur and night prayer.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Bilal pronounces the Adhan at night so that you may eat and drink till Ibn Um Maktum pronounces the Adhan (for the Fajr prayer)
question: What is the significance of the timing of the Adhan in relation to eating and drinking before Fajr prayer?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by explaining the significance of the timing of the Adhan in relation to eating and drinking before Fajr prayer.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) finished his prayer after offerings two rak`at only. Dhul-Yaddain asked him whether the prayer had been reduced, or you had forgotten?" The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Is Dhul-Yaddain speaking the truth?" The people said, "Yes." Then Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) stood up and performed another two rak`at and then finished prayer with Taslim, and then said the Takbir and performed a prostration similar to or longer than his ordinary prostrations; then he raised his head, said Takbir and prostrated and then raised his head (Sahu prostrations)
question: What should be done if a mistake is made during the prayer?
verification_reason: The answer explains the correct procedure to follow when a mistake is made during prayer, which is to add the missed rak'at and perform the Sahu prostrations.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:While the people were at Quba offering the morning prayer, suddenly a person came to them saying, "Tonight Divine Inspiration has been revealed to Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) and he has been ordered to face the Ka`ba (in prayers): therefore you people should face it." There faces were towards Sham, so they turned their faces towards the Ka`ba (at Mecca)
question: What is the significance of the change in the Qibla direction in Islamic history?
verification_reason: The text describes the change in Qibla direction from Sham to Ka'ba, which is the significance of the event.

hadith-eng: Narrated Al-Bara':When Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) arrived at Medina, he prayed facing Jerusalem for sixteen or seventeen months but he wished that he would be ordered to face the Ka`ba. So Allah revealed: -- 'Verily! We have seen the turning of your face towards the heaven; surely we shall turn you to a prayer direction (Qibla) that shall please you.' (2.144) Thus he was directed towards the Ka`ba. A man prayed the `Asr prayer with the Prophet (ﷺ) and then went out, and passing by some people from the Ansar, he said, "I testify. that I have prayed with the Prophet (ﷺ) and he (the Prophet) has prayed facing the Ka`ba." Thereupon they, who were bowing in the `Asr prayer, turned towards the Ka`ba
question: What was the initial direction of prayer for Muslims before facing the Ka'ba?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by stating the initial direction of prayer was Jerusalem, and then later changed to the Ka'ba.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas bin Malik:I used to offer drinks prepared from infused dates to Abu Talha Al-Ansari, Abu 'Ubada bin Al Jarrah and Ubai bin Ka`b. Then a person came to them and said, "All alcoholic drinks have been prohibited." Abu Talha then said, "O Anas! Get up and break all these jars." So I got up and took a mortar belonging to us, and hit the jars with its lower part till they broke
question: What actions did the companions take upon learning about the prohibition of alcoholic drinks?
verification_reason: The answer describes the actions taken by the companions upon learning about the prohibition of alcoholic drinks.

hadith-eng: Narrated Hudhaifa:The Prophet (ﷺ) said to the people of Najran, "I will send to you an honest person who is really trustworthy." The Companion, of the Prophet (ﷺ) each desired to be that person, but the Prophet (ﷺ) sent Abu 'Ubaida
question: What qualities did the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) emphasize when selecting a representative for a task?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by describing the qualities the Prophet emphasized and the action taken to demonstrate those qualities.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "For every nation there is an Amin (honest, trustworthy person) and the Amin of this nation is Abu 'Ubaida
question: Who is considered the trustworthy person for the Muslim nation according to the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ)?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by mentioning the name of the trustworthy person.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Umar:There was a man from the Ansar (who was a friend of mine). If he was not present in the company of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) I used to be present with Allah's Messenger (ﷺ), I would tell him what I used to hear from Allah's Messenger (ﷺ), and when I was absent from Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) he used to be present with him, and he would tell me what he used to hear from Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)
question: How did companions of the Prophet (ﷺ) ensure they shared knowledge of his teachings with each other?
verification_reason: The text describes a scenario where the narrator shares knowledge of the Prophet's teachings with a companion who was absent, and vice versa, ensuring the knowledge is shared among them.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Ali:The Prophet (ﷺ) , sent an army and appointed some man their commander The man made a fire and then said (to the soldiers), "Enter it." Some of them intended to enter it while some others said, 'We have run away from it (i.e., embraced Islam to save ourselves from the 'fire')." They mentioned that to the Prophet, and he said about people who had intended to enter the fire. ''If they had entered it, they would have remained In it till the Day of Resurrection.'' Then he said to others, "No obedience for evil deeds, obedience is required only in what is good
question: What is the Islamic perspective on obeying commands that involve evil deeds?
verification_reason: The answer clearly states the Islamic perspective on obeying commands that involve evil deeds, which is that obedience is only required in what is good.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira and Zaid bin Khalid:Two men sued each other before the Prophet
question: What is the process followed when two individuals have a legal dispute in Islamic tradition?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by mentioning a scenario of two individuals having a legal dispute and taking it to the Prophet.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:While we were with Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) a bedouin got up and said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! Settle my case according to Allah's Book (Laws)." Then his opponent got up and said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! He has said the truth! Settle his case according to Allah's Book (Laws.) and allow me to speak," He said, "My son was a laborer for this man and he committed illegal sexual intercourse with his wife. The people told me that my son should be stoned to death but I ransomed him with one-hundred sheep and a slave girl. Then I asked the religious learned people and they told me that his wife should be stoned to death and my son should receive one-hundred lashes and be sentenced to one year of exile.' The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "By Him in Whose Hands my life is, I will judge between you according to Allah's Book (Laws): As for the slave girl and the sheep, they are to be returned; and as for your son, he shall receive onehundred lashes and will be exiled for one year. You, O Unais!" addressing a man from Bani Aslam, "Go tomorrow morning to the wife of this (man) and if she confesses, then stone her to death." The next morning Unais went to the wife and she confessed, and he stoned her to death
question: How does Islamic law address cases of illegal sexual intercourse and what are the prescribed punishments?
verification_reason: The text accurately describes the prescribed punishment for illegal sexual intercourse, as per Islamic law, which includes stoning to death for the adulteress and lashes and exile for the adulterer.

hadith-eng: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah:On the day of (the battle of) the Trench, the Prophet (ﷺ) called the people (to bring news about the enemy). Az-Zubair responded to his call. He called them again and Az-Zubair responded to his call again; then he called them for the third time and again Az-Zubair responded to his call whereupon the Prophet said, "Every prophet has his Hawairi (helper), and Az-Zubair is my Hawari
question: What qualities make someone a close companion or helper of a prophet?
verification_reason: The text describes the qualities of being a close companion or helper of a prophet, specifically mentioning Az-Zubair's prompt response to the Prophet's calls.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Musa:The Prophet (ﷺ) entered a garden and told me to guard its gate. Then a man came and asked permission to enter. The Prophet, said, "Permit him and give him the good news that he will enter Paradise." Behold! It was Abu Bakr. Then `Umar came, and the Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Admit him and give him the good news that he will enter Paradise." Then `Uthman came and the Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Admit him and give him the good news that he will enter Paradise
question: What significance does the Prophet's assurance of Paradise hold for Abu Bakr, Umar, and Uthman?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by mentioning the Prophet's assurance of Paradise for Abu Bakr, Umar, and Uthman.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Umar:I came and behold, Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) was staying on a Mashroba (attic room) and a black slave of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) was at the top if its stairs. I said to him, "(Tell the Prophet) that here is `Umar bin Al- Khattab (asking for permission to enter)." Then he admitted me
question: What is the significance of seeking permission before entering someone's private space in Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The text illustrates the practice of seeking permission before entering someone's private space, as demonstrated by the Prophet's servant asking Umar to seek permission before entering.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Abbas:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) sent a letter to Khosrau and told his messenger to give it first to the ruler of Bahrain, and tell him to deliver it to Khosrau. When Khosrau had read it, he tore it into pieces. (Az-Zuhri said: I think Ibn Al-Musaiyab said, "Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) invoked Allah to tear them (Khosrau and his followers) into pieces
question: What was the reaction of Khosrau upon receiving the letter from the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ)?
verification_reason: The answer describes the reaction of Khosrau upon receiving the letter from the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ)

hadith-eng: Narrated Salama bin Al-Akwa`:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said to a man from the tribe of Al-Aslam, "Proclaim among your people (or the people) on the day of 'Ashura' (tenth of Muharram), 'Whosoever has eaten anything should fast for the rest of the day; and whoever has not eaten anything, should complete his fast
question: What instructions did the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) give regarding fasting on the day of 'Ashura'?
verification_reason: The answer directly addresses the question about the Prophet's instructions regarding fasting on the day of 'Ashura'.

hadith-eng: Narrated Ibn `Abbas:When the delegate of `Abd Al-Qais came to Allah's Messenger (ﷺ), he said, "Who are the delegate?" They said, "The delegate are from the tribe of Rabi`a." The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Welcome, O the delegate, and welcome! O people! Neither you will have any disgrace nor will you regret." They said, "O Allah's Apostle! Between you and us there are the infidels of the tribe of Mudar, so please order us to do something good (religious deeds) that by acting on them we may enter Paradise, and that we may inform (our people) whom we have left behind, about it." They also asked (the Prophet) about drinks. He forbade them from four things and ordered them to do four things. He ordered them to believe in Allah, and asked them, "Do you know what is meant by belief in Allah?" They said, "Allah and His Apostle know best." He said, ''To testify that none has the right to be worshipped except Allah, the One, Who has no partners with Him, and that Muhammad is Allah's Messenger (ﷺ); and to offer prayers perfectly and to pay Zakat." (the narrator thinks that fasting in Ramadan is included), "and to give one-fifth of the war booty (to the state)." Then he forbade four (drinking utensils): Ad-Duba', Al56 Hantam, Al-Mazaffat and An-Naqir, or probably, Al-Muqaiyar. And then the Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Remember all these things by heart and preach it to those whom you have left behind
question: What are the fundamental beliefs and practices that lead to entering Paradise according to Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The text describes the fundamental beliefs and practices that lead to entering Paradise, as mentioned by the Prophet (ﷺ) to the delegate of `Abd Al-Qais.

hadith-eng: Narrated Tauba Al-`Anbari:Ash-'Shu`bi asked me, "Did you notice how Al-Hasan used to narrate Hadiths from the Prophets? I stayed with Ibn `Umar for about two or one-and-half years and I did not hear him narrating any thing from the Prophet (ﷺ) except his (Hadith): He (Ibn `Umar) said, "Some of the companions of the Prophet (ﷺ) including Sa`d, were going to eat meat, but one of the wives of the Prophet (ﷺ) called them, saying, 'It is the meat of a Mastigure.' The people then stopped eating it. On that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, 'Carry on eating, for it is lawful.' Or said, 'There is no harm in eating it, but it is not from my meals
question: What is the significance of the Prophet's companions seeking his guidance on dietary matters?
verification_reason: The answer provides a specific example of the Prophet's companions seeking his guidance on dietary matters.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Abdullah:The Prophet (ﷺ) led us in Zuhr prayer and prayer five rak`at. Somebody asked him whether the prayer had been increased." He (the Prophet (ﷺ) ) said, "And what is that?" They (the people) replied, "You have prayed five rak`at." Then the Prophet (ﷺ) offered two prostrations (of Sahu) after he had finished his prayer with the Taslim

hadith-eng: Narrated Malik:We came to the Prophet (ﷺ) and we were young men nearly of equal ages and we stayed with him for twenty nights. Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) was a very kind man and when he realized our longing for our families, he asked us about those whom we had left behind. When we informed him, he said, "Go back to your families and stay with them and teach them (religion) and order them (to do good deeds). The Prophet (ﷺ) mentioned things some of which I remembered and some I did not. Then he said, "Pray as you have seen me praying, and when it is the time of prayer, one of you should pronounce the call (Adhan) for the prayer and the eldest of you should lead the prayer
question: What guidance did the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) provide on how to perform prayers and lead them?
verification_reason: The text provides guidance on how to perform prayers and lead them, as instructed by the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ)

hadith-eng: Narrated Ibn Mas`ud:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "The (call for prayer) Adhan of Bilal should not stop anyone of you from taking his Suhur for he pronounces the Adhan in order that whoever among you is praying the night prayer, may return (to eat his Suhur) and whoever among you is sleeping, may get up, for it is not yet dawn (when it is like this)." (Yahya, the sub-narrator stretched his two index fingers side ways)
question: What guidance is provided regarding the consumption of Suhur before the dawn prayer?
verification_reason: The text provides guidance on when to take Suhur in relation to the Adhan of Bilal.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Bilal pronounces the Adhan at night so that you may eat and drink till Ibn Um Maktum pronounces the Adhan (for the Fajr prayer)
question: What role does the Adhan play in regulating the pre-dawn activities of Muslims during Ramadan?
verification_reason: The text explains the role of Adhan in regulating pre-dawn activities by indicating the time when eating and drinking should stop.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) finished his prayer after offerings two rak`at only. Dhul-Yaddain asked him whether the prayer had been reduced, or you had forgotten?" The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Is Dhul-Yaddain speaking the truth?" The people said, "Yes." Then Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) stood up and performed another two rak`at and then finished prayer with Taslim, and then said the Takbir and performed a prostration similar to or longer than his ordinary prostrations; then he raised his head, said Takbir and prostrated and then raised his head (Sahu prostrations)
question: What is the significance of performing Sahu prostrations in prayer?
verification_reason: The answer explains the significance of Sahu prostrations in prayer, which is to complete the prayer after forgetting or missing a part of it.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:While the people were at Quba offering the morning prayer, suddenly a person came to them saying, "Tonight Divine Inspiration has been revealed to Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) and he has been ordered to face the Ka`ba (in prayers): therefore you people should face it." There faces were towards Sham, so they turned their faces towards the Ka`ba (at Mecca)
question: What does the immediate response of the people at Quba indicate about their faith and obedience?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by describing the immediate response of the people at Quba to the revelation.

hadith-eng: Narrated Al-Bara':When Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) arrived at Medina, he prayed facing Jerusalem for sixteen or seventeen months but he wished that he would be ordered to face the Ka`ba. So Allah revealed: -- 'Verily! We have seen the turning of your face towards the heaven; surely we shall turn you to a prayer direction (Qibla) that shall please you.' (2.144) Thus he was directed towards the Ka`ba. A man prayed the `Asr prayer with the Prophet (ﷺ) and then went out, and passing by some people from the Ansar, he said, "I testify. that I have prayed with the Prophet (ﷺ) and he (the Prophet) has prayed facing the Ka`ba." Thereupon they, who were bowing in the `Asr prayer, turned towards the Ka`ba
question: What does the change in Qibla direction signify about the relationship between divine guidance and prophetic wishes?
verification_reason: The answer text explains the change in Qibla direction and its relation to the Prophet's wishes and divine guidance.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas bin Malik:I used to offer drinks prepared from infused dates to Abu Talha Al-Ansari, Abu 'Ubada bin Al Jarrah and Ubai bin Ka`b. Then a person came to them and said, "All alcoholic drinks have been prohibited." Abu Talha then said, "O Anas! Get up and break all these jars." So I got up and took a mortar belonging to us, and hit the jars with its lower part till they broke
question: What is the significance of immediate compliance with Islamic prohibitions as shown by the companions?
verification_reason: The text illustrates immediate compliance with Islamic prohibitions as shown by Abu Talha Al-Ansari's actions after being informed of the prohibition.

hadith-eng: Narrated Hudhaifa:The Prophet (ﷺ) said to the people of Najran, "I will send to you an honest person who is really trustworthy." The Companion, of the Prophet (ﷺ) each desired to be that person, but the Prophet (ﷺ) sent Abu 'Ubaida
question: How did the companions of the Prophet (ﷺ) react to the prospect of being chosen for a significant responsibility?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by describing the reaction of the companions and the action taken by the Prophet (ﷺ)

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "For every nation there is an Amin (honest, trustworthy person) and the Amin of this nation is Abu 'Ubaida
question: Why is trustworthiness emphasized in the leadership of a nation in Islam?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by mentioning the importance of trustworthiness in a nation's leadership.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Umar:There was a man from the Ansar (who was a friend of mine). If he was not present in the company of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) I used to be present with Allah's Messenger (ﷺ), I would tell him what I used to hear from Allah's Messenger (ﷺ), and when I was absent from Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) he used to be present with him, and he would tell me what he used to hear from Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)
question: How did the companions of the Prophet (ﷺ) collaborate to preserve and transmit his teachings?
verification_reason: The text describes a method of collaboration among companions to preserve and transmit the Prophet's teachings.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Ali:The Prophet (ﷺ) , sent an army and appointed some man their commander The man made a fire and then said (to the soldiers), "Enter it." Some of them intended to enter it while some others said, 'We have run away from it (i.e., embraced Islam to save ourselves from the 'fire')." They mentioned that to the Prophet, and he said about people who had intended to enter the fire. ''If they had entered it, they would have remained In it till the Day of Resurrection.'' Then he said to others, "No obedience for evil deeds, obedience is required only in what is good
question: What are the consequences of following commands that contradict Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The text explains the consequences of following commands that contradict Islamic teachings, specifically the consequences of intending to do evil deeds.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira and Zaid bin Khalid:Two men sued each other before the Prophet
question: What role does the Prophet play in resolving disputes according to Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The text directly addresses the role of the Prophet in resolving disputes, as indicated by the context of a lawsuit being brought before him.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:While we were with Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) a bedouin got up and said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! Settle my case according to Allah's Book (Laws)." Then his opponent got up and said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! He has said the truth! Settle his case according to Allah's Book (Laws.) and allow me to speak," He said, "My son was a laborer for this man and he committed illegal sexual intercourse with his wife. The people told me that my son should be stoned to death but I ransomed him with one-hundred sheep and a slave girl. Then I asked the religious learned people and they told me that his wife should be stoned to death and my son should receive one-hundred lashes and be sentenced to one year of exile.' The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "By Him in Whose Hands my life is, I will judge between you according to Allah's Book (Laws): As for the slave girl and the sheep, they are to be returned; and as for your son, he shall receive onehundred lashes and will be exiled for one year. You, O Unais!" addressing a man from Bani Aslam, "Go tomorrow morning to the wife of this (man) and if she confesses, then stone her to death." The next morning Unais went to the wife and she confessed, and he stoned her to death
question: What role does confession play in the implementation of Islamic legal punishments?
verification_reason: The text explains how confession plays a role in the implementation of Islamic legal punishments, specifically in the case of adultery.

hadith-eng: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah:On the day of (the battle of) the Trench, the Prophet (ﷺ) called the people (to bring news about the enemy). Az-Zubair responded to his call. He called them again and Az-Zubair responded to his call again; then he called them for the third time and again Az-Zubair responded to his call whereupon the Prophet said, "Every prophet has his Hawairi (helper), and Az-Zubair is my Hawari
question: What significance does the title 'Hawari' hold in Islamic tradition?
verification_reason: The answer directly addresses the significance of the title 'Hawari' by explaining its meaning and context in Islamic tradition.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Musa:The Prophet (ﷺ) entered a garden and told me to guard its gate. Then a man came and asked permission to enter. The Prophet, said, "Permit him and give him the good news that he will enter Paradise." Behold! It was Abu Bakr. Then `Umar came, and the Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Admit him and give him the good news that he will enter Paradise." Then `Uthman came and the Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Admit him and give him the good news that he will enter Paradise
question: What role did Abu Musa play in the event where the Prophet gave glad tidings of Paradise to certain individuals?
verification_reason: The text mentions Abu Musa as the one guarding the gate, and the Prophet giving glad tidings of Paradise to Abu Bakr, Umar, and Uthman.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Umar:I came and behold, Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) was staying on a Mashroba (attic room) and a black slave of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) was at the top if its stairs. I said to him, "(Tell the Prophet) that here is `Umar bin Al- Khattab (asking for permission to enter)." Then he admitted me
question: What can be learned about the Prophet's living conditions from this narration?
verification_reason: The narration provides insight into the Prophet's living conditions, specifically that he was staying in a simple attic room.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Abbas:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) sent a letter to Khosrau and told his messenger to give it first to the ruler of Bahrain, and tell him to deliver it to Khosrau. When Khosrau had read it, he tore it into pieces. (Az-Zuhri said: I think Ibn Al-Musaiyab said, "Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) invoked Allah to tear them (Khosrau and his followers) into pieces
question: What was the consequence mentioned in the hadith for Khosrau's action of tearing the letter?
verification_reason: The hadith mentions that Allah's Messenger invoked Allah to tear Khosrau and his followers into pieces as a consequence of Khosrau's action of tearing the letter.

hadith-eng: Narrated Salama bin Al-Akwa`:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said to a man from the tribe of Al-Aslam, "Proclaim among your people (or the people) on the day of 'Ashura' (tenth of Muharram), 'Whosoever has eaten anything should fast for the rest of the day; and whoever has not eaten anything, should complete his fast
question: What guidance is provided for those who have not eaten on the day of 'Ashura'?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by providing guidance for those who have not eaten on the day of 'Ashura'.

hadith-eng: Narrated Ibn `Abbas:When the delegate of `Abd Al-Qais came to Allah's Messenger (ﷺ), he said, "Who are the delegate?" They said, "The delegate are from the tribe of Rabi`a." The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Welcome, O the delegate, and welcome! O people! Neither you will have any disgrace nor will you regret." They said, "O Allah's Apostle! Between you and us there are the infidels of the tribe of Mudar, so please order us to do something good (religious deeds) that by acting on them we may enter Paradise, and that we may inform (our people) whom we have left behind, about it." They also asked (the Prophet) about drinks. He forbade them from four things and ordered them to do four things. He ordered them to believe in Allah, and asked them, "Do you know what is meant by belief in Allah?" They said, "Allah and His Apostle know best." He said, ''To testify that none has the right to be worshipped except Allah, the One, Who has no partners with Him, and that Muhammad is Allah's Messenger (ﷺ); and to offer prayers perfectly and to pay Zakat." (the narrator thinks that fasting in Ramadan is included), "and to give one-fifth of the war booty (to the state)." Then he forbade four (drinking utensils): Ad-Duba', Al56 Hantam, Al-Mazaffat and An-Naqir, or probably, Al-Muqaiyar. And then the Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Remember all these things by heart and preach it to those whom you have left behind
question: How important is it to convey religious teachings to others in Islam?
verification_reason: The text emphasizes the importance of conveying religious teachings to others, as the Prophet instructs the delegate to remember the teachings and preach them to those they have left behind.

hadith-eng: Narrated Tauba Al-`Anbari:Ash-'Shu`bi asked me, "Did you notice how Al-Hasan used to narrate Hadiths from the Prophets? I stayed with Ibn `Umar for about two or one-and-half years and I did not hear him narrating any thing from the Prophet (ﷺ) except his (Hadith): He (Ibn `Umar) said, "Some of the companions of the Prophet (ﷺ) including Sa`d, were going to eat meat, but one of the wives of the Prophet (ﷺ) called them, saying, 'It is the meat of a Mastigure.' The people then stopped eating it. On that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, 'Carry on eating, for it is lawful.' Or said, 'There is no harm in eating it, but it is not from my meals
question: What can be inferred about the dietary preferences of the Prophet from this narration?
verification_reason: The narration provides insight into the Prophet's dietary preferences by describing a situation where he allows his companions to eat a type of meat that was initially considered forbidden.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Abdullah:The Prophet (ﷺ) led us in Zuhr prayer and prayer five rak`at. Somebody asked him whether the prayer had been increased." He (the Prophet (ﷺ) ) said, "And what is that?" They (the people) replied, "You have prayed five rak`at." Then the Prophet (ﷺ) offered two prostrations (of Sahu) after he had finished his prayer with the Taslim
question: What should be done if a mistake is made during the prayer?
